title: Debugger workflow

publication.changeSummary: Topic-specific draft for Debugger workflow; requires human source, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Software Engineering; August 2023; section applicable to Debugger workflow
sources.claim: A debugger pauses controlled execution to inspect frames, variables, and control flow against a stated hypothesis, using breakpoints and stepping as observations rather than random exploration.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A debugger pauses controlled execution to inspect frames, variables, and control flow against a stated hypothesis, using breakpoints and stepping as observations rather than random exploration.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A breakpoint before a failing division confirms the denominator becomes zero only on empty input, supporting one specific guard hypothesis. Your goal is to record and explain a debugger-assisted root-cause investigation. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Dependencies and reproducible builds; Commits and history; pf tools debug test; Build and task commands. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Operational model
sections.body: A debugger pauses controlled execution to inspect frames, variables, and control flow against a stated hypothesis, using breakpoints and stepping as observations rather than random exploration. The learner should use this model to predict an observable result rather than merely repeat terminology.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure point
sections.body: Breakpoints alter timing, optimized code can reorder or remove variables, and debugger state does not replace a reproducible test or explain external-system behavior. A correct explanation therefore includes the applicable assumptions and avoids this common mistake: The best debugging strategy is to step through every line from program start until something looks suspicious.

includes: Set breakpoints, step, inspect variables and test a hypothesis rather than editing blindly.
Record and explain a debugger-assisted root-cause investigation.
excludes: Breakpoints alter timing, optimized code can reorder or remove variables, and debugger state does not replace a reproducible test or explain external-system behavior.

outcomes: Record and explain a debugger-assisted root-cause investigation.

examples.title: Debugger workflow: direct case
examples.situation: A breakpoint before a failing division confirms the denominator becomes zero only on empty input, supporting one specific guard hypothesis.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the starting evidence: A breakpoint before a failing division confirms the denominator becomes zero only on empty input, supporting one specific guard hypothesis.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Check the result against this correction: Reproduce narrowly, state what observation would support or refute a hypothesis, place targeted breakpoints, and preserve the exposing case as a test.
examples.result: The case supports the declared outcome because Reproduce narrowly, state what observation would support or refute a hypothesis, place targeted breakpoints, and preserve the exposing case as a test.

examples.title: Debugger workflow: boundary case
examples.situation: Stepping into a helper reveals an argument is already wrong at the call boundary, moving investigation to the caller rather than the helper body.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed context: Stepping into a helper reveals an argument is already wrong at the call boundary, moving investigation to the caller rather than the helper body.

Step 2 — Keep this boundary explicit: Breakpoints alter timing, optimized code can reorder or remove variables, and debugger state does not replace a reproducible test or explain external-system behavior.
Step 3 — Reject the documented misconception: The best debugging strategy is to step through every line from program start until something looks suspicious.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Record and explain a debugger-assisted root-cause investigation.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: The best debugging strategy is to step through every line from program start until something looks suspicious.
misconceptions.correction: Reproduce narrowly, state what observation would support or refute a hypothesis, place targeted breakpoints, and preserve the exposing case as a test.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using the direct and boundary scenarios, explain why this belief fails: The best debugging strategy is to step through every line from program start until something looks suspicious.

analogy.example: A controlled investigation can model Debugger workflow: preserve evidence, state a hypothesis, inspect one clue, automate repeatable checks, and narrow the change point.
usefulFor: Linking debugging, static checks, history search, and automated local gates
limitations: Debuggers and hooks can alter timing or execute code, and tool findings remain bounded evidence rather than proof.
Breakpoints alter timing, optimized code can reorder or remove variables, and debugger state does not replace a reproducible test or explain external-system behavior.

recap.summary: You should now be able to record and explain a debugger-assisted root-cause investigation. Keep this boundary in view: Breakpoints alter timing, optimized code can reorder or remove variables, and debugger state does not replace a reproducible test or explain external-system behavior.
retrievalPrompts: Explain Debugger workflow without using the optional analogy and include one observable consequence.
Why is this statement incorrect: The best debugging strategy is to step through every line from program start until something looks suspicious.
Apply the model to this case from memory: Stepping into a helper reveals an argument is already wrong at the call boundary, moving investigation to the caller rather than the helper body.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Formatter and linter”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
